name: ProShares Ultra Bloomberg Crude Oil
description: 2x daily leveraged exposure to Bloomberg WTI Crude Oil Subindex. The go-to instrument for leveraged crude oil bets.
bullCase: Amplifies daily oil returns by 2x. Call options on UCO provide leverage-on-leverage, potentially 4-6x effective exposure to oil price moves.
bearCase: Leveraged decay erodes value in choppy markets. Contango in oil futures creates persistent drag. Daily reset means long-term returns diverge from 2x cumulative.
strategy: Buy ATM or slightly OTM calls 60-90 days out. The built-in 2x leverage means options give 4-6x effective oil exposure.
notes: Top pick for short-term leveraged crude oil bull trades. Options chains are liquid with tight spreads.

name: Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exp & Prod Bull 2X
description: 2x daily leveraged exposure to S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index. Tracks oil producer stocks, not crude directly.
bullCase: Oil producers have operational leverage on top of the 2x ETF leverage. When oil rises, producer margins expand dramatically. Triple-layer leverage with call options.
bearCase: Producer stocks can underperform crude due to hedging, cost inflation, or capital discipline. 2x daily decay applies.
strategy: Buy calls 60-90 days out. Producer leverage + 2x ETF leverage + options leverage = massive upside potential if oil rallies.
notes: Offers exposure to oil producer equities with 2x leverage. Producers have natural operational leverage on oil prices.

name: Direxion Daily Energy Bull 2X Shares
description: 2x daily leveraged exposure to the Energy Select Sector Index. Broader energy exposure including integrated majors, refiners, and services.
bullCase: Diversified energy exposure with 2x leverage. Less volatile than pure E&P plays. Includes mega-caps like XOM and CVX that benefit from rising oil.
bearCase: Broader exposure dilutes pure oil upside. Includes companies with mixed commodity exposure. Daily leverage decay.
strategy: Good for a broad energy bull bet. Buy calls 60-90 days out for leveraged exposure across the entire energy sector.
risks: Diversified exposure dilutes pure oil play
notes: More diversified than GUSH. Good if you want broad energy leverage rather than pure crude or E&P.

name: ProShares Ultra Oil & Gas
description: 2x daily leveraged exposure to the Dow Jones U.S. Oil & Gas Index. Similar to ERX but tracks a different index.
bullCase: 2x leveraged play on oil & gas equities. Solid options market. Benefits from rising commodity prices across oil and natural gas.
bearCase: Daily leverage decay. Mixed oil and gas exposure means natural gas weakness can offset oil strength.
strategy: Buy ATM calls 60-90 days out. Use as alternative to ERX for leveraged energy sector exposure.
risks: Natural gas exposure may dilute pure oil play
notes: Alternative to ERX. Check options liquidity before trading - spreads can be wider.

name: United States Oil Fund LP
description: Tracks WTI crude oil via front-month futures. The most popular unleveraged oil ETF. Massive options market.
bullCase: Direct crude oil exposure with the most liquid options market of any oil ETF. Tight bid-ask spreads on options enable precise positioning.
bearCase: No built-in leverage. Contango in futures erodes long-term returns. Has historically underperformed spot crude over time.
strategy: Buy deep OTM calls for maximum leverage on a 100% oil move. The high options liquidity allows for aggressive strike selection with reasonable spreads.
notes: Best options liquidity of any oil ETF. For maximum leverage, use deep OTM calls. Spreads are tight even on far OTM strikes.

name: United States Brent Oil Fund LP
description: Tracks Brent crude oil futures. Brent is the global benchmark and often trades at a premium to WTI.
bullCase: Brent reflects global oil pricing better than WTI. Geopolitical premiums show up more in Brent. Good for playing international supply disruptions.
bearCase: Lower options liquidity than USO. No built-in leverage. Same contango issues as other oil futures ETFs.
strategy: Use for Brent-specific trades. Options liquidity is decent but check spreads before entering.
notes: Play Brent specifically when you expect geopolitical premiums or WTI-Brent spread moves.

name: Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund
description: The benchmark energy sector ETF. Holds major integrated oil companies, E&P firms, and service companies. Extremely liquid options.
bullCase: Most liquid energy sector ETF with massive options market. Stable, diversified energy exposure. Great for LEAPs if you want long-dated leveraged bets.
bearCase: No built-in leverage. Heavy weighting toward XOM and CVX means it moves slower than pure-play oil instruments.
strategy: Buy LEAP calls (12+ months) for long-term leveraged energy bet. XLE options are extremely liquid with tight spreads even on long-dated contracts.
notes: Best-in-class options liquidity for energy. LEAP calls are the play here for long-term oil bulls.

name: SPDR S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production ETF
description: Equal-weighted E&P ETF. More exposure to small/mid-cap producers than XLE. Higher beta to oil prices.
bullCase: Equal weighting gives more exposure to smaller producers with higher operational leverage. Higher beta than XLE means bigger moves when oil rallies.
bearCase: More volatile. Small-cap E&P companies carry higher individual risk. No built-in leverage.
strategy: Buy OTM calls 3-6 months out. The higher beta means you get more oil price sensitivity per dollar of premium.
risks: Equal weighting means exposure to weaker names
notes: Higher beta than XLE. The equal-weight approach means small producers can drive outsized returns.

name: VanEck Oil Services ETF
description: Tracks oil field services companies (SLB, HAL, BKR). Services companies have high operational leverage to drilling activity.
bullCase: Oil services companies benefit from increased drilling activity that comes with higher oil prices. High operational leverage means earnings can surge.
bearCase: Services lag oil price moves - there's a delay. Capital discipline by producers limits drilling growth even when oil rises.
strategy: Buy calls 3-6 months out to capture the delayed benefit of rising oil prices on services activity.
notes: Oil services are a second-derivative play. They benefit when higher oil prices lead to more drilling activity.

name: Exxon Mobil Corporation
description: Largest US oil company. Integrated operations spanning upstream, downstream, and chemicals. Acquired Pioneer Natural Resources in 2024.
bullCase: Dominant position with massive production base. Pioneer acquisition added top-tier Permian Basin assets. Strong free cash flow funds buybacks and dividends.
bearCase: Mega-cap means slower moves. Downstream and chemicals exposure dilutes pure upstream oil leverage. Already well-owned by institutions.
strategy: Sell cash-secured puts or buy LEAP calls. XOM options are extremely liquid. Consider bull call spreads to reduce cost.
notes: The blue-chip oil play. Lower beta but extremely liquid options. Good for conservative leveraged bets via LEAPs.

name: Occidental Petroleum Corporation
description: Major E&P company with significant Permian Basin position. Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway holds ~28% stake. Also invested in direct air capture (DAC) technology.
bullCase: High operational leverage to oil prices. Buffett's massive stake signals long-term value. CrownRock acquisition added premium Permian acreage. Debt reduction improving balance sheet.
bearCase: Higher debt load than peers. DAC investment is a capital sink with uncertain returns. Permian exposure is great but concentration risk exists.
strategy: OXY has high beta to oil. Buy OTM calls 3-6 months out for maximum leverage. The Buffett backing provides a floor.
notes: Buffett-backed, high-beta oil play. One of the best pure-play E&P options for leveraged upside.

name: Chevron Corporation
description: Second-largest US integrated oil company. Strong position in Permian Basin and international assets. Pending Hess Corporation acquisition adds Guyana exposure.
bullCase: Hess acquisition adds world-class Guyana assets with low breakeven costs. Strong shareholder returns via buybacks and dividends. Disciplined capital allocation.
bearCase: Hess deal faces arbitration risk with CNOOC. Mega-cap limits upside velocity. Integrated model dilutes pure upstream leverage.
strategy: Buy LEAP calls or bull call spreads. CVX options are very liquid. Good for a long-term oil bull thesis.
notes: Premium integrated oil company. Lower beta than E&P pure plays but extremely liquid options.

name: ConocoPhillips
description: Largest independent E&P company. Pure upstream focus with global operations. Acquired Marathon Oil in 2024, adding US unconventional assets.
bullCase: Pure upstream focus means direct oil price leverage. Marathon Oil acquisition scaled up US operations. Industry-leading low cost of supply. Strong shareholder returns.
bearCase: Already large-cap, limiting upside velocity. Acquisition integration risk. International exposure adds geopolitical complexity.
strategy: Buy calls 3-6 months out. COP is the largest pure-play upstream company - direct oil price leverage with quality assets.
notes: Best large-cap pure upstream play. Higher oil beta than XOM/CVX because no downstream dilution.

name: Devon Energy Corporation
description: Mid-cap E&P focused on US unconventional basins. Pioneer of the variable dividend model. Strong Permian and Delaware Basin position.
bullCase: Variable dividend model means massive cash returns when oil prices are high. Mid-cap size offers more upside velocity than mega-caps. Quality acreage position.
bearCase: Variable dividend cuts both ways - drops significantly when oil falls. Mid-cap concentration risk. US-only exposure.
strategy: Buy OTM calls 3-6 months out. DVN's variable dividend model means the stock surges when oil rises. High beta play.
catalysts: Oil price rally boosts variable dividend
notes: Variable dividend model amplifies stock moves with oil prices. Great for leveraged call option plays.

name: Diamondback Energy Inc
description: Pure-play Permian Basin E&P company. One of the lowest-cost producers in the basin. Acquired Endeavor Energy in 2024.
bullCase: Lowest-cost Permian producer with massive inventory. Endeavor acquisition doubled its position. High margins at any oil price above $50. Pure oil leverage.
bearCase: Permian Basin concentration. Large Endeavor deal needs integration. Stock already reflects premium valuation.
strategy: Buy calls 3-6 months out. FANG is one of the highest-quality E&P names with direct oil price leverage.
notes: Best-in-class Permian pure play. The Endeavor deal made it a top-tier E&P company.

name: EOG Resources Inc
description: Premium E&P company known for technical excellence and capital discipline. Multi-basin US operations with industry-leading returns.
bullCase: Best-in-class capital efficiency. Multi-basin diversification reduces concentration risk. Consistently finds high-return drilling locations. Strong balance sheet.
bearCase: Premium valuation limits upside. Capital discipline means slower production growth. Already well-owned.
strategy: Buy calls 3-6 months out. EOG is a quality play with solid oil price leverage and lower risk than smaller E&Ps.
notes: Quality premium play. Lower beta than DVN or OXY but higher quality and lower risk.

name: Marathon Petroleum Corporation
description: Largest US independent refiner. Benefits from crack spreads (difference between crude oil and refined product prices). Also owns MPLX midstream.
bullCase: Refiners benefit from both oil price recovery and strong product demand. Crack spreads can surge during supply disruptions. Massive buyback program.
bearCase: Refining margins are independent of absolute oil price - crack spreads matter more. Cyclical business. Regulatory risk on emissions.
strategy: Not a pure oil price play. Use only if you also expect strong gasoline/diesel demand and wide crack spreads.
notes: Refining play, not pure oil. Best when oil rises AND product demand is strong. Less direct oil price leverage.

name: Schlumberger Limited (SLB)
description: Largest oil field services company globally. Provides drilling, completion, production, and digital solutions. Strong international exposure.
bullCase: Rising oil prices drive increased drilling activity, boosting services demand. International spending cycle is early innings. Digital and AI solutions adding growth.
bearCase: Services lag oil price moves by 6-12 months. Producer capital discipline limits drilling growth. Cyclical business.
strategy: Buy calls 6-12 months out to capture the lagged benefit of rising oil prices on services spending.
notes: Second-derivative oil play via services. Best for longer-dated bullish bets where oil stays high.

name: Halliburton Company
description: Second-largest oil field services company. More North America-focused than SLB. Strong in completion and production services.
bullCase: Higher North America exposure means faster response to US oil activity. Smaller than SLB with more upside velocity. Strong in completions technology.
bearCase: North America rig count has been declining. Services companies face pricing pressure. Cyclical business.
strategy: Buy calls 3-6 months out if you expect US drilling activity to increase with rising oil prices.
notes: More US-levered than SLB. Higher beta to North American oil activity.

name: APA Corporation
description: Mid-cap E&P with US (Permian), international (Suriname, Egypt, North Sea), and exploration upside. Higher risk/reward profile.
bullCase: Suriname exploration could be transformational. High beta to oil prices. Cheaper valuation than peers. Significant upside if oil rallies.
bearCase: Suriname is pre-revenue and uncertain. International operations add complexity. Higher debt and risk profile.
strategy: Buy OTM calls for maximum leverage. APA is a high-beta name that moves aggressively with oil prices.
notes: High risk/reward E&P play. Suriname exploration is a potential game-changer.

name: Ovintiv Inc
description: Mid-cap E&P operating in Permian, Montney (Canada), and Uinta basins. Formerly Encana. Oil-weighted production with multi-basin diversification.
bullCase: Multi-basin diversification with quality assets. Cheaper valuation than Permian pure plays. Strong free cash flow generation. Oil-weighted production.
bearCase: Canadian operations add currency and regulatory risk. Not the highest-quality acreage. Mixed perception from Encana rebrand.
strategy: Buy calls 3-6 months out for a value-oriented oil bull play with multi-basin diversification.
notes: Value E&P play with multi-basin diversification. Trades at a discount to Permian pure plays.

name: Coterra Energy Inc
description: Dual-basin E&P with Permian oil and Marcellus natural gas. The 2021 merger of Cabot Oil & Gas and Cimarex Energy.
bullCase: Dual commodity exposure provides optionality. Permian oil assets benefit from rising crude. Marcellus gas provides diversification and LNG export tailwind.
bearCase: Gas exposure dilutes pure oil play. Dual-basin model adds complexity. Mixed commodity pricing can create confusing signals.
strategy: Buy calls if you're bullish on both oil AND natural gas. Less ideal as a pure crude oil play.
risks: Natural gas price weakness dilutes returns
notes: Oil + gas combo. Best when both commodities are rising. Less ideal for a pure oil bull thesis.

name: Chord Energy Corporation
description: Williston Basin (Bakken) focused E&P formed from the Whiting Petroleum and Oasis Petroleum merger. Pure-play Bakken operator.
bullCase: Bakken pure play with significant acreage position. Smaller cap offers more upside velocity. Variable return framework returns cash aggressively.
bearCase: Bakken-only concentration risk. Smaller scale than Permian peers. Lower liquidity options market.
strategy: Buy OTM calls for high-beta Bakken exposure. Check options liquidity first - spreads may be wide.
notes: Bakken pure play with high variable dividend. Lower options liquidity - use limit orders.

name: Baker Hughes Company
description: Third-largest oil field services company. Diversified across oilfield services, industrial & energy technology. Publishes the Baker Hughes rig count.
bullCase: More diversified than SLB/HAL with industrial energy technology exposure. LNG equipment business is a secular growth driver. Cheaper valuation.
bearCase: Diversification dilutes pure oil services leverage. Industrial segment creates noise. Transition period between old and new energy.
strategy: Buy calls 6-12 months out for diversified energy services exposure. LNG equipment cycle is a bonus catalyst.
notes: Most diversified of the big three services companies. LNG equipment is the unique angle.

name: Valero Energy Corporation
description: Largest independent refiner by capacity. Strong in renewable diesel. Benefits from wide crack spreads and complex refining operations.
bullCase: Complex refineries earn premium margins. Renewable diesel provides growth and ESG narrative. Massive buyback program drives per-share value.
bearCase: Refining margins independent of crude oil price direction. Crack spreads can compress quickly. Renewable diesel margins under pressure.
strategy: Not a pure oil play. Use only if you also expect strong refined product demand and wide crack spreads.
notes: Refining play, not pure crude oil. Crack spreads matter more than absolute oil price.

name: Phillips 66
description: Diversified downstream energy company. Refining, midstream, chemicals, and marketing. Recently converted Rodeo refinery to renewable fuels.
bullCase: Diversified downstream model with multiple earnings streams. Midstream provides stability. Marketing provides margin capture through the value chain.
bearCase: Downstream diversification means limited pure oil price leverage. Rodeo conversion was costly. Refining margins are cyclical.
strategy: Not ideal for a pure oil price bet. Better for a broader energy sector play.
notes: Downstream company - limited direct crude oil price leverage. Better plays exist for oil bull thesis.

name: Marathon Oil Corporation
description: Now part of ConocoPhillips (acquired 2024). Previously a mid-cap E&P focused on Eagle Ford, Bakken, and Permian.
bullCase: If still trading independently, was a high-beta E&P with quality US assets. Now part of COP.
bearCase: Acquisition by COP completed. May no longer trade independently.
strategy: Trade COP instead for exposure to the combined MRO + COP asset base.
notes: Acquired by ConocoPhillips in 2024. Trade COP instead.

name: Antero Resources Corporation
description: Appalachian natural gas and NGL producer. One of the largest US natural gas producers. Benefits from LNG export growth.
bullCase: NGL component provides some oil price sensitivity. LNG export growth is a secular tailwind for US gas. Cheap valuation.
bearCase: Primarily natural gas, not oil. Gas prices are depressed. Limited crude oil price leverage.
strategy: Not ideal for a pure crude oil bull thesis. Better plays exist if you're specifically betting on oil.
notes: Primarily a natural gas play. Only relevant to oil thesis through NGL exposure.

name: WTI Crude Oil Futures
description: The benchmark US crude oil futures contract traded on NYMEX/CME. Each contract = 1,000 barrels. Margin requirements provide built-in leverage of ~10-15x.
bullCase: Direct crude oil exposure with built-in margin leverage. No tracking error, no expense ratios, no contango drag. The purest way to bet on oil prices.
bearCase: Margin calls can force position liquidation. Each $1 move = $1,000 per contract. High leverage magnifies losses. Requires futures-approved account.
strategy: Buy crude oil call options directly on futures. This is the purest and most leveraged way to bet on oil. Options on futures provide defined risk with massive leverage.
notes: The purest oil play. Options on CL futures are the ultimate leveraged oil bet. Requires a futures account.

name: Brent Crude Oil Futures
description: The global benchmark crude oil futures contract traded on ICE. Each contract = 1,000 barrels. Brent typically trades at a premium to WTI.
bullCase: Global benchmark price. Captures geopolitical risk premiums better than WTI. Options on Brent futures provide pure global oil exposure.
bearCase: Same leverage risks as WTI futures. Slightly lower liquidity in US trading hours. Requires futures account.
strategy: Buy Brent call options if you expect geopolitical premiums or global supply disruptions. WTI is more liquid for US traders.
notes: Global oil benchmark. Use when playing international supply disruptions or geopolitical events.

name: ProShares Ultra Bloomberg Natural Gas
description: 2x daily leveraged exposure to Bloomberg Natural Gas Subindex. Not crude oil, but often correlated and relevant to broader energy thesis.
bullCase: If your energy bull thesis extends to natural gas, BOIL provides 2x leveraged exposure. LNG export growth is a secular tailwind.
bearCase: Natural gas ≠ crude oil. Different supply/demand dynamics. Extreme contango drag in nat gas futures. Very volatile.
strategy: Only use if you're also bullish on natural gas. Not a crude oil play. Extreme leverage decay risk.
notes: Natural gas play, not crude oil. Only relevant if your thesis extends to gas. Extreme decay risk.

name: C3.ai Inc
description: Enterprise AI platform with specific solutions for oil & gas. Applications include predictive maintenance, production optimization, and reservoir management.
bullCase: AI adoption in oil & gas is accelerating. C3.ai has established oil industry partnerships. If oil companies invest more in AI to optimize production, C3.ai benefits.
bearCase: Not a direct oil price play. Revenue is from software, not commodities. Competitive AI market. Unprofitable.
strategy: This is an AI-in-oil play, not a direct oil price play. Only use if you believe AI spending by oil companies will increase.
notes: AI technology for oil companies. Indirect oil play through technology spending.

name: Palantir Technologies Inc
description: Data analytics and AI platform used by oil majors for operational optimization. Foundry platform deployed across energy sector.
bullCase: Palantir's Foundry platform is used by BP, oil services companies, and others for data-driven decision making. AI spending in oil is growing.
bearCase: Not an oil play at all. Revenue comes from government and commercial software. Oil is a tiny segment. Very expensive valuation.
strategy: Not recommended for an oil price thesis. Palantir is an AI/tech play that happens to serve some oil clients.
notes: AI platform used by some oil companies. Not a direct oil investment.

name: TechnipFMC plc
description: Subsea and surface technology provider for the oil & gas industry. Leader in deepwater equipment and services. Benefits from offshore drilling cycle.
bullCase: Deepwater/subsea spending is in a multi-year upcycle. FTI is the dominant subsea player. High barriers to entry. Record backlog levels.
bearCase: Long cycle business - benefits are delayed. Capital intensive. Offshore is more expensive than US unconventional.
strategy: Buy calls 6-12 months out to capture the deepwater spending upcycle. FTI benefits from sustained high oil prices.
notes: Best pure play on deepwater/subsea spending cycle. Multi-year tailwind from offshore development.

name: Weatherford International plc
description: Mid-cap oil field services company. Emerged from bankruptcy in 2019. Focused on drilling, completions, and production optimization.
bullCase: Post-bankruptcy company with clean balance sheet. Mid-cap size offers upside velocity. International growth opportunity. Cheaper than SLB/HAL.
bearCase: Post-bankruptcy stigma. Smaller scale limits competitive positioning. Lower liquidity.
strategy: Buy calls 3-6 months out for a higher-beta services play. Check options liquidity - may need to use wider strikes.
notes: Higher-risk services play with more upside potential than big three. Check options liquidity.
